description:
Confused about which health insurance suits your needs? Book a free chat with Ditto for spam-free, personalized advice - https://ditto.sh/f0actz

Welcome to our channel! In today’s video, we’re comparing two popular health insurance policies: ICICI Elevate by ICICI Lombard Health Insurance and Tata AIG Medicare Premier by Tata Health Insurance. These policies are some of the most discussed options in the market, and we’re here to help you decide which one might be the right fit for your needs. Whether you’re looking for affordability, advanced features, or comprehensive coverage, we’ve got you covered.

Key Takeaways:
By the end of this video, you’ll learn:
- The key differences between ICICI Elevate and Tata AIG Medicare Premier.
- Unique features like Infinite Care, Power Booster, and worldwide coverage.
- Pros, cons, and the ideal customer for each policy.
- Why other insurers like Aditya Birla, HDFC Ergo, and Care Supreme might be worth considering.

Claim Settlement Ratio & Hospital Networks
ICICI Lombard: CSR of 85% with 7,500+ hospitals.
Tata AIG: CSR of 96% with an impressive 10,000+ hospitals.

Coverage Options
ICICI Elevate: Coverage for surrogate mother and Oocyte donor (₹5 lakhs), add-ons like Infinite Care (no sum insured limit), and Power Booster (100% cumulative bonus annually). Room rent is limited to a single private room.
Tata AIG Medicare Premier: Coverage up to ₹3 crores with no room rent limits, worldwide coverage (₹10 lakhs), and maternity benefits (₹60,000 for girl child, ₹50,000 for boy child after 4 years).

Shared Features
 Both policies offer:
Day care treatment coverage
Pre and post-hospitalization
Ambulance charges
Free health checkups
AYUSH and organ donor coverage

Cons to Watch Out For:
ICICI Elevate: Lower CSR, fewer network hospitals, and room rent restrictions.
Tata AIG Medicare Premier: Higher premiums, unnecessary built-in features, and a long maternity waiting period.

Add-Ons & Customization
ICICI Elevate: Highly customizable with add-ons like Infinite Care, Power Booster, and maternity cover (10% of the base sum insured).
Tata AIG Medicare Premier: Most features are built into the base plan, but this results in higher premiums.

Conclusion
So, which one should you choose? If you prioritize comprehensive coverage and better service metrics, Tata AIG Medicare Premier might be your pick. But if you’re looking for affordability and customizable features, ICICI Elevate by ICICI Lombard Health Insurance could be the better option.

Our recommendation? Look into insurers like Aditya Birla, HDFC Ergo, and Care Supreme, which offer more comprehensive features.

Make the best choice based on your specific needs, whether it’s network size, budget, or advanced benefits like maternity coverage or worldwide access.

Need help choosing the right policy? Book a free consultation with Ditto for spam-free, expert guidance. Don’t miss out— https://ditto.sh/f0actz

comments.text:
Here are some features of Tata AIG Medicare:
In-patient treatment: Covers expenses for hospitalization due to illness, injury, or disease during the policy period 
Pre-hospitalization: Covers medical expenses incurred within 60 days before hospitalization 
Post-hospitalization: Covers medical expenses incurred for a specified number of days after discharge 
Domiciliary treatment: Covered 
Second opinion: Covered 
Ambulance cover: Covered 
Hearing aid: Covered 
Daily cash: Covered for accompanying an insured child or for choosing shared accommodation 
AYUSH benefit: Covered 
Health checkup: Covered 
Organ donor: Covered 
In-patient dental treatment: Covered 
Vaccination cover: Covered 
Prolonged hospitalization benefit: Covered 
Bariatric surgery: Covered 
Delivery complications cover: Covered
